interactions.summary: The host keeps spiraling after the interview, mocking electoral priorities, urging membership support, and admitting that everything feels meaningless except a vague turn toward spirituality.
A producer or off-mic colleague checks whether Jiang wants to leave to speak with his team, and the exit is confirmed.
After Jiang exits, the producer reads viewer comments aloud, including speculation about digital currency, criminal cabals, rapture theology, tax refusal, Greater Israel, and anti-British terrorism.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Producer reading live chat or donor comments

speaker_notes.note: The speaker after seg-0163 appears to be a producer or secondary host managing the transition and then reading chat, not Jiang.

chronology_notes.note: This packet is a post-interview transition: the host signs toward the next show, Jiang exits, and the stream pivots from dialogue into audience-comment reading.

uncertainty_notes.note: The quoted comment stream is noisy and should not be treated as Jiang claims; the important semantic fact is that the show has shifted into reading audience reactions.
